idk_sentence: I don't know based on the provided Medium articles data.
notes: Manually curated from load_articles(limit=100). Questions are intentionally semantic: most non-IDK tests use body anchors that are not copied from titles or tags.

tests.question: Find an article about Phineas Gage. Provide the title and author.
expected_titles: Surviving a Rod Through the Head
tests.rubric: Must name 'Surviving a Rod Through the Head' by Rishav Sinha and identify it as the article about Phineas Gage.

tests.question: Find an article about banned antibacterial compounds that still show up in ordinary homes and bodies. Provide the title and author.
expected_titles: The FDA Banned These Chemicals — and They’re Still Everywhere
tests.rubric: Must name 'The FDA Banned These Chemicals — and They’re Still Everywhere' by Maryn Mckenna and mention banned antibacterial chemicals such as triclosan or triclocarban.

tests.question: Find an article about a psychosis-risk signal involving how people use words related to sound. Provide the title and author.
expected_titles: AI analyzes language to predict schizophrenia
tests.rubric: Must name 'AI analyzes language to predict schizophrenia' by Phil Siarri and connect it to language patterns that predict psychosis risk.

tests.question: Find an article about a smartphone system that can estimate an object's size and orientation in real time. Provide the title and author.
expected_titles: Google Objectron — A giant leap for the 3D object detection
tests.rubric: Must name 'Google Objectron — A giant leap for the 3D object detection' by Jair Ribeiro and mention real-time 3D object detection.

tests.question: Find an article about intermittent fasting improving memory. Provide the title and author.
tests.rubric: Must return the exact IDK sentence because the first 100 articles do not contain an article about intermittent fasting improving memory.

tests.question: Find an article about training an overlooked sense to change the brain, and summarize its central argument.
expected_titles: Mind Your Nose
tests.rubric: Must identify 'Mind Your Nose' and summarize smell training, olfactory plasticity, and brain adaptation.

tests.question: Find an article that discusses Proust, Chekhov, and Kafka while questioning what kind of sound environment helps creativity, and summarize its central argument.
expected_titles: For Creatives, Silence Isn’t Always Golden
tests.rubric: Must identify 'For Creatives, Silence Isn’t Always Golden' and explain why silence is not always the best creative environment.

tests.question: Find an article about how AI could help businesses become more circular, and summarize the main argument.
expected_titles: Unleash the Potential of AI in Circular Economy: Businesses Potentials
tests.rubric: Must identify the circular-economy AI article and summarize business opportunities such as environmental monitoring, data analysis, or product innovation.

tests.question: Find an article that pushes back on calling low motivation laziness, and summarize its central argument.
expected_titles: ‘Am I Just Lazy?’

tests.question: Find an article that argues social media algorithms are the main cause of teenage sleep loss, and summarize its central argument.
tests.rubric: Must return the exact IDK sentence because adjacent mental-health or productivity articles do not make this argument.
